narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school and then hearing you'll likely struggle with the bar exam feels like a gut punch, especially with your passion for law shining through in your target choice. Our model looked at your profile and saw a 63% chance of not passing the bar, painting a picture of someone who's fought hard but faces steep odds based on patterns from thousands of similar paths. The biggest signal came from your Year 3 decile of 3, which stood out as the strongest pull toward difficulty, suggesting that even as you progressed, staying in the lower third of your class raised real flags about handling the bar's intensity. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile of 2, reinforcing that early challenges in keeping up with peers set a tough foundation. Your LSAT score of 27 added to the concern, as it positioned you below the levels that typically power through the exam's demands. Even with your upper-middle family income offering some stability, it didn't quite offset the hurdles, becoming another factor tipping toward struggle. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.4 echoed those academic patterns, while being a white male in a part-time track layered on subtle risks seen in past data. It's not about your worth—it's this specific mix creating a risky story for bar success. The good news? You're early enough to rethink: boost that LSAT, aim higher in deciles next time, or go full-time for focus. You've got the drive; channel it to rewrite this narrative. We're rooting for you to prove the odds wrong.
